The Desert Tech MDR (Micro Dynamic Rifle) is a versatile, bullpup-configured rifle known for its compact design and exceptional modularity. Chambered for various calibers, it excels in both close-quarters and long-range combat. Its ambidextrous controls, lightweight frame, and high accuracy make it a reliable choice for those navigating the unpredictable dangers of the Zone. This version is chambered in 7.62x51 with a black polymer body.

One of the most technologically advanced bodysuits in the Zone. The ACE armor system incorporates a next-generation CBRN and a pocketed Kevlar vest with Class III plate inserts. Using state of the art materials such as selectively-permeable coatings, this suit manages both maximal anomaly protection and wearer comfort. Used by only the wealthiest Mercenaries.\n \n

A personalized letter of appreciation recognizing your contributions to science, personally signed by Academician Skabinsky. Also includes a recommendation to accept the bearer of this document as a candidate at the Chernobyl Zonal Institute.

The .45 calibre full metal jacket round has enjoyed popularity for a hundred years thanks to its ballistic performance and a relatively small propelling charge. As a result, the bullet in this round is relatively slow but highly accurate and provides considerable stopping power.

The .338 Lapua Magnum is a specialised rimless bottlenecked centrefire cartridge developed for military long-range sniper rifles. The Afghanistan War and Iraq War made it a combat-proven round with ready and substantial ammunition availability. The .338 Lapua is a dual-purpose anti-personnel and anti-materiel round, but its anti-materiel potential is limited.

The HK 4.6x30mm cartridge is a type of ammunition primarily used in the Heckler & Koch MP7 personal defense weapon (PDW). It is designed to minimize weight and recoil while increasing penetration of body armor. It features a bottlenecked case and a pointed, steel-core, brass-jacketed bullet.

The 5.7x28mm was developed in conjunction with the FN P90 personal defense weapon (PDW) and FN Five-seven pistol, in response to NATO requests for a replacement for the 9x19mm Parabellum cartridge.

The .50 Browning Machine Gun (12.7x99mm NATO and designated as the 50 Browning by the C.I.P.) is a cartridge developed for the Browning .50 caliber machine gun in the late 1910s, entering official service in 1921. Under STANAG 4383, it is a standard cartridge for NATO forces as well as many non-NATO countries. The cartridge itself has been made in many variants: multiple generations of regular ball, tracer, armor-piercing (AP), incendiary, and saboted sub-caliber rounds. The rounds intended for machine guns are made into a continuous belt using metallic links. 
The .50 BMG cartridge is also used in long-range target and anti-materiel rifles, as well as other .50-caliber machine guns. 
 
%c[ui_gray_2] - %c[ui_gray_2] Armor penetration power (AP): %c[ui_gray_1]BR6

The 7.62x25mm cartridge is a Russian bottle-necked pistol cartridge widely used in former Soviet satellite states, China and Pakistan among other countries. A Lot of these rounds are still available in the Zone. This 7.62x25mm Tokarev cartridge comprises a bullet with lead round nose, polymer coated (LRNPC) bullet intended for sport shooting and hunting. By design, this cartridge provides a considerable stopping power effect as well as causing substantial adverse effects on the target after impact, this is at the cost of penetration capabilities, even against basic ballistic protection.

The steel core 7.62x25mm cartridge is a Russian bottle-necked pistol cartridge widely used in former Soviet satellite states, China and Pakistan among other countries. This 7.62x25mm Tokarev cartridge displays a bullet with steel core (Pst) and a steel sleeve. It's very effective against medium armored targets and is thus appreciated in the Zone.

7.62x39 M43 is an armour piercing round produced in China, which is why it's often referred to as "7.62x39 CN". This round features a mild steel core which enhances its penetration capabilities. This Chinese round (as well as all other M43 ammunition) is currently banned for import in the US because US federal law classifies the round as an armour-piercing handgun round. This classification is based on materials and bullet design rather than on empirical ability to penetrate armour.

7.62x39 Full Metal Jacket is one of the most commonly used types of ammunition, especially in Africa and in the Middle East. Some nicknamed it the "Round of the Revolution" due to its favored use by both terrorist and criminal organizations. Due to the sheer power of the round most military organizations have turned to using additional steel plates to counter 7.62x39 as there is a high probability of encountering it in combat situations.

At present, it is most commonly employed by private military contractors all over the world. The 7.62x51 round is a very common machine gun calibre, and can also be used in battle or sniper rifles.

The hardened steel heads tend to better penetrate hard objects compared to other 7.62x54r rounds, but at the cost of soft targets over penetration. The reduced grains also tend to have a lower impact and thus reduced internal damages compared to standard sniper variants of 7.62x54r ammunition.

A stolen military exoskeleton customized by outlaws to attack well-guarded facilities and to plunder well-armed stalkers. More martially inclined aspiring Bandit Lords tend to favor this armor, using it as a stark and unquestionable symbol of their strength and authority over lesser criminals.\n \n

Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. Both imposing and highly intimidating in appearance, this outfit is a favorite among the Zone's most powerful outlaws.\n \n
A 'Sunset' bandit suit with the vest switched out for a special pocketed load-bearing alternative. A solid suit for a scavenger on a budget.\n \n

Jackets like this first entered circulation among Bandits thanks to Quartermaster Morgan, a corrupt supplier who formerly belonged to the Duty faction. Offers protection comparable to a standard Military service outfit. An inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n

A black leather jacket commonly associated with the Zone's bandits and crooks. Mostly worn to intimidate rookies from other factions, it provides very little protection. An inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n

Armor like this first entered circulation among Bandits thanks to Quartermaster Morgan, a corrupt supplier who formerly belonged to the Duty faction. It gives up some of its physical protection for greater comfort and defense against anomalies. These suits are a major sore point for the Duty faction, and getting them out of the hands of anarchists, criminals and murderers is an unspoken priority.\n \n

A SEVA-like suit made from scratch by one of the Bandits' gifted craftsmen. Compared to the looted SEVAs that are brought in and restored, this patchwork outfit boasts average protection against anomalies while retaining a surprisingly low weight. Additionally, it's rather stylish and comfy, allowing it to become a hit among the criminals who can afford it.\n \n

Bandits have never been terribly interested in the science and research of anomalous fields, but nevertheless, need protective suits such as these to organize ambushes and attacks on stalkers within anomalous fields and radiation pockets.\n \n

A Zone-assembled suit, made by local craftsmen. Its design bears resemblance to a Monolith X-18 suit with its conventional NBC features stripped down in favor of greater insulation from thermal and electrical threats. Mostly used by Bandits in Dark Valley to clear out the poltergeists that occasionally escape from the local lab.\n \n

A light combat suit obtained to help armor local Bandit gangs. Its design is based on the suit used by the special forces of Western armies. In a twist of dramatic irony, it appears whoever supplied these suits were ripping off their customers as the build quality is significantly worse.\n \n
A special case with loot for the Bartender.

A flight data recorder, or "black box". Flight recorders are used to record the status of internal systems and crew conversations and can provide vital information in the event of an accident. In the Zone, stalker and military personnel alike are interested in these devices.

"The Zone is a miracle. The Masters cleansed us. Soon, we thought, the Zone would cleanse the world. But no. Our small paradise was soon trespassed upon by the flawed people of a sinful world. And, in an act of unthinkable atrocity, the beast named Strelok crippled the valiant Monolith, and entered the Masters' chambers to slay their mortal forms. However, I shall not despair. I cannot let this miracle be consumed by the vice and filth of the unworthy. So we, Sin, must embark upon a quest to fulfil the Masters' wish. We shall wage war upon the violent to bring about eternal peace."

Some notes about old men's conspiracy theories.
 
 There are rumors that the Agroprom Institute was used as a front for an unofficial lab in the years preceding the 2006 accident. This lab hosted a number of very unusual experiments. The traders have dug up a lot of information about these laboratories.
 I met with the Barkeep. He says he sent Duty scouts to get into the underground vaults of the Dark Valley, called X-18, but they disappeared. There are other rumors regarding a 'psi-brain' mechanism. The whole neighborhood in Yantar is under the influence of that device. It zombified everyone who came within range. There is another mechanism in the Red Forest radar underground, called lab X-19. That is likely to be the Brain Scorcher.
 There is evidence to suggest that the presence of the Brain Scorcher intentionally blocks the road to the North, but no one knows exactly what it is. The Barkeep believes that the Brain Scorcher is man-made, and these mechanisms are not always operating at full strength. He thinks there are periods when their power is lowered to cool down the apparatus. It's time to visit all the underground laboratories in the Zone and find out what kind of secrets they are hiding. I'm too old for this shit. I wish Strelok was still around...

Special device for studying anomalous fields, belonging to the group Clear Sky.
A device used by the Outcasts of the Monolith to listen in on the fanatics' conversations.

An experimental version of the army exoskeleton smuggled into the Zone from Ukrainian factories before being refurbished by Clear Sky technicians. Generally, suits like these are held in reserve, to be deployed only in times of true crisis. That said, especially trusted comrades and associates of the faction may eventually receive permission to acquire such a suit for their own personal uses, although not without great cost.\n \n

Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. These outfits are smuggled in from Ukrainian factories near the borders of the Zone.\n \n
Encrypted information related to the Clear Sky faction is stored in this PDA.
A CS-2b stalker suit with the vest switched out for a special pocketed load-bearing alternative. A solid suit for a scavenger on a budget.\n \n
An unofficial version of the SEVA scientific suit, fresh off the smuggling route. Typically used by groups of the Clear Sky faction.\n \n

An unofficial version of the SEVA scientific suit that has been extensively improved by Novikov, Clear Sky's resident technician. Extremely comfortable to move around in, making it a high-demand suit among some factions.\n \n

Unlike other suits in the CS-1, 2 and 3 series, this body armor was only created after the faction's massive losses at the Chernobyl NPP. It is widely considered to be the first proper successor to the CS-1 suit as it focuses on keeping the wearer safe from anomalous environments over armoring them for battle.\n \n

A set of CS-2b body armor that has been specially modified to fit with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it provides less protection but is also considerably lighter.\n \n

Formerly the standard outfit issued to Clear Sky assault squads, after the faction’s infamous failure against the NPP in 2011, this armor serves as a reminder that even an excellent protective bodysuit cannot guarantee safety on the wrong side of the perimeter.\n \n
A light suit worn by Clear Sky's novice researchers. It provides more than adequate protection from the anomalous phenomena found within the Swamps.\n \n
An upgrade to the CS-1 bodysuit intended for conducting rapid search operations in areas of low risk.\n \n
A heavier but still comfortable outfit worn by members of Clear Sky that provides more than adequate protection, especially in combat.\n \n

A soft blue leather anorak worn over a turtleneck sweater, tucked into jeans and hiking boots. These simple suits are assembled in large quantities by Clear Sky's tailors for use within the faction. An inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n

This unassuming military-grade radio can intercept and decrypt nearby transmissions.

Excellent high-tech armor consisting of a steel-reinforced Kevlar jumpsuit and a tactical vest with Class IV inserts, all carried by powered limbs and servomotors. Domestically developed by one of the research institutes, specifically for the Duty faction. It is slowly being phased out in favor of cheaper, more practical suits, and the few Exosuits in active service tend to be in the hands of Duty's oldest veterans.\n \n

Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. This suit is typically found in the hands of elite Duty warriors, especially those assigned to guard VIPs or launch exceedingly dangerous raids on enemy stalkers.\n \n

Developed by defence research institutes in Kiev, the first iteration of this Duty bodysuit began as a simple old set of Spetsnaz PSZ-9 body armor, modified for the Zone. These days, this armor's latest design is capable of stopping a rifle bullet and boasts great protection against the Zone's myriad horrors. It is uncommon for armor as prized as this to be issued to any Duty member who hasn't proven themselves in some significant manner.\n \n

A PSZ-9d armored bodysuit, specially designed to be fitted with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it sacrifices raw ballistic protection for greater functionality in anomalous fields.\n \n

This suit was a favorite of the late Lieutenant Colonel Shulga, who rarely ventured outside his operational base in Yanov, yet constantly feared assassination at the hands of Freedom. Thus, heavy protection like this fitted his needs perfectly. As fate would have it, he'd instead later be presumed dead after leading a mass assault into Pripyat with his contingent of troops. Veteran Duty member will occasionally wear suits like this to battle in his honor.\n \n

A dark leather anorak worn over a turtleneck sweater, tucked into jeans and combat boots. Formerly used only as relaxed wear, these simple suits were never intended for use by those on active service. An inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n
An old version of the Duty bodysuit, better suited for recruits who may prefer the familiarity of stalker suits over frontline battle armor.\n \n

An "in-between" suit that is more protective than Duty's regular PS5 universal protection suit, yet cheaper and easier to produce than a proper set of PSZ-9d body armor. Ideal for skirmishes, its relative ease of maintenance and repair is a great boon to Dutyers far away from friendly territory, such as the investigators who venture up into Yanov or beyond.\n \n

A PS5-M universal protection bodysuit, specially designed to be fitted with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it provides less protection but is also considerably lighter.\n \n

A somewhat heavier variant of the PS5-M universal protection suit, specifically designed for Duty and intending to hunt mutants. A small amount of ballistic protection is sacrificed in favor of much better protection against common mutant attacks. Compares well even to modern Duty suits.\n \n

The Duty faction version of the standard SEVA suit. Like the standard suit variant, it consists of a durable anomaly-resistant CBRN jumpsuit and a specially-treated Class II Kevlar vest. A solid suit for extended dives into anomaly fields.\n \n

A lighter variant of the Duty scientific suit that has shed almost half its weight at the cost of slightly decreased protection. The ballistic armor has been replaced by a class IIA vest with numerous pockets.\n \n

As a token of goodwill and to help cover their research costs, Clear Sky occasionally makes coveted "Pathfinder" suits on commission for use among the Duty faction. Altered according to Duty's needs, its weight has been reduced by almost half at the expense of protection. Despite this, it's an excellent and comfortable outfit for Duty's more curious members.\n \n

An old Duty suit developed alongside the PS5-M universal protection outfit intended to protect Duty scouts in the early days of the Zone's existence. However, the only parts of the Zone left to be explored are far too dangerous for a light bodysuit like this. As such, these are mostly retired, kept in reserve only to outfit Duty recruits in desperate circumstances.\n \n

A set of former Spetsnaz body armor, bolstered with more substantial protection. An excellent combat bodysuit, although it has an infamous failure rate in areas of extreme anomalous activity. Despite this, Duty's veterans claim you do not need protection from anomalies once you know how to spot them.\n \n

A modern, updated variant of the original PS5-M universal protection bodysuit. It is more than adequate for all of the Zone's conditions as well as being markedly comfortable for a Duty suit. Originally intended to replace its predecessor entirely, its higher production cost put this goal on hold in the face of Duty's multitude of recent losses.\n \n

"Finally, after several years of arduous preparation, we can continue the Masters' plan. It's regrettable that so many people don't believe us, but sometimes words aren't enough. They're blinded by base human desires. If they insist on continuing their violent ways, we'll treat them with violence. Maybe then they will understand. The seed of Vyraj is budding, and I'm here to spread the paradise further and further beyond. I am the tip of the spear."

When out in the field, wearers of this suit are more commonly used to carrying ludicrous amounts of batteries, LLMCs, AAMs and other supplies an accompanying scientist might need when exploring the Zone.\n \n

A unique armor system composed of an exoskeleton-powered SSP-99M jumpsuit with an anomaly-resistant Class IIA Kevlar vest placed on top. The durable exo-frames are capable of providing mechanical support for carrying heavy loads even through anomaly fields. Ideal for aiding researchers during deep incursions into the Zone.\n \n

A basic suit originally intended as spare equipment for Professor Hermann's "Iskra Scientific Research and Investigation Team", it has since become a relatively cheap protective bodysuit for Ecologist guards working all over the Zone.\n \n
Military expedition report from the North of the Zone.

Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. This variant includes an old CBRN jumpsuit lined with Kevlar and an additional attachment slot.\n \n

A next generation exoskeleton, consisting of a doubly reinforced Exosuit. Rumored to have been the brainchild of a now-deceased stalker named "Fang", whose designs were later claimed by all sorts of powerful factions across the Zone. If regular exoskeletons allow stalkers to feel like a light vehicle, the Nosorog almost makes them believe they're a main battle tank, able to withstand incredible punishment while carrying tremendous ordnance.\n \n

This suit is designed to simulate a ‘heap of foliage.’ Usually, this type of suit is worn by snipers and hunters to blend in with the environment and hide themselves from enemies. Ghillie suits are commercially produced, but usually both military personnel and civilians customize and improve them to suit their needs.\n \n
A more advanced variant of regular Sin flak armor, typically gifted to the faction's more loyal and devout members. These suits do a great job fortifying the already durable warriors of Sin.\n \n

A comfortable and ergonomic arming suit used to outfit Sin's followers en masse. It consists of a ballistic vest over an environmental bodysuit. These suits do a good job fortifying the already durable warriors of Sin.\n \n

High-tech armor consisting of a steel-reinforced Kevlar jumpsuit and a tactical vest with Class IV inserts, all carried by powered limbs and servomotors. Study samples have revealed that its servos are made of alloys and materials unknown to any field of science in any other part of the world. Wearing this suit creates an unnatural feeling of bliss and calm within the wearer, although whether this sense of security is genuine or not remains to be understood.\n \n

This outfit's design is based on the PS5-M universal protection suit, though it is unknown how it has come into the possession of Sin. Nevertheless, Duty is not happy to know their armor designs are being appropriated by Zone worshiping fanatics and the recovery of these suits are an unspoken priority. The suit itself is of good quality and is more than adequate for the Zone's conditions.\n \n

A long, thick, bloodstained overcoat worn over a butchered scientist suit. Although the closed-cycle system and several other features seem to have been stripped off, the end result still compares favorably to many basic stalker suits.\n \n

The ACH-7 (Advanced Compound Headgear, 7 Parts) is a more modern version of the surplus steel helmet. Though, its lack of a respiratory device makes it an extremely poor choice for the Zone's conditions.\n \n

The ACH-7 (Advanced Compound Headgear, 7 Parts) is a more modern version of the surplus steel helmet. The ACH-7EX comes with the inclusion of a respiratory system, boasting chemical and radiological protection somewhere between that of a GP-5 and a PBF kit.\n \n
An M50 gas mask tweaked for better functionality in the Zone. Overall, it is considered an improvement over the M40.\n \n
A Russian gas mask well suited for CBRN ecological hazards. In the Zone, it can be considered a functional and spiritual successor to the GP-5.\n \n
Using the GP-10 as its model, this gas mask was designed primarily to protect the wearer from Controllers and Poltergeists.\n \n

A helmet commissioned by extremely influential stalkers, based off the design of the 'Apocalypse' helmet formerly used by the faction, "Final Day". In comparison, this helmet is lighter and more protective than its predecessor, comparing favorably to modern combat helmets such as the 'Sphere' series.\n \n

A suit created by one of Kiev's defense research institutes for the purpose of guarding researchers in the Zone. However, it is clear the suit was created on a budget. As such, none of these suits were ever officially commissioned by the Ukrainian government, although a few managed to find their way into the Zone.\n \n

The official version of the UNISG exoskeleton released in limited quantities by special order of the United Nations. While it still possesses many of the design flaws of other exoskeletons, it's nonetheless seen as a great alternative to light command and scout vehicles.\n \n

Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. This particular outfit is of high quality and feels exceptionally light and comfortable to wear. A next generation exoskeleton, consisting of a doubly reinforced Exosuit. Rumored to have been the brainchild of a now-deceased stalker named "Fang", whose designs were later claimed by all sorts of powerful factions across the Zone. If regular exoskeletons allow stalkers to feel like a light vehicle, the Nosorog almost makes them believe they're a main battle tank, able to withstand incredible punishment while carrying tremendous ordnance.\n \n

A warm leather anorak worn over a turtleneck sweater, tucked into jeans and hiking boots. Formerly owned by a typical firearm enthusiast, it sports several advantages over an ordinary jacket, namely a camouflage pattern, higher quality material and more pockets to hold belongings. Still an inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n

A warm leather anorak worn over a turtleneck sweater, tucked into jeans and hiking boots. The outfit of choice among naive or inexperienced stalkers. An inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n

A warm leather anorak with an integrated backpack, worn over a turtleneck sweater, tucked into jeans and hiking boots. It seems the previous owner really loved hiking. The educated choice of materials and weight management system makes this more useful than most leather jackets. Still an inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n
Heavily encrypted documents impossible to crack without the right cipher.
Among the many entries there is one very interesting one: "Strider has assembled a new group to fight the Monolith. Rumor has it that among his men are former members of Lebedev's strike team."

The manuscript contains a brief report detailing how Fang and Strelok infiltrated a hidden laboratory and the journey they took to reach it. It mentions a significant Monolith presence in and around the Chernobyl NPP, particularly beneath the concrete sarcophagus, suggesting it could be their main base of operations, possibly hiding something of importance. Further along, the report describes the discovery of another laboratory further north, beneath the generators, though they were unable to gain access at the time due to the need for a special decoder.\n \n

The contents of this package are unknown.

 %c[d_orange] • %c[pda_white] Quest Item
A sturdy device that was planted on a mutant to monitor its movements, habits and vital signs. Shows signs of corrosion, and is covered in numerous bite and scratch marks.
A snazzy bright red leather anorak worn over a turtleneck sweater, tucked into jeans and hiking boots. An inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n

One of a few exoskeletons assembled by the Renegades back in 2011 to help secure their final push into Clear Sky's base. Unfortunately, they were virtually wiped out before their final victory ever came, thanks to the interference of a Mercenary with a trench coat and a marksman rifle. Modern Renegades who wear this armor always go into battle like a man possessed, eager to mete vengeance upon their most hated foes.\n \n

This comfortable bodysuit, originally intended for research in the Zone, makes for a great alternative to the various other light combat or hand-made suits readily available within the Zone. Compared to a standard SEVA suit, it provides improved protection against thermal hazards and blunt trauma. However, its other protective qualities are inferior to the original SEVA.\n \n

A light combat suit obtained through shady means to help armor local groups of Renegades. Its design is based on the suit used by the special forces of Western armies. Surprisingly pleasant to wear.\n \n

Designed and produced by one of Kiev's defense research institutes and represents an excellent alternative to hand-made stalker suits. It combines a CBRN jumpsuit with a specially-treated class II Kevlar vest. Due to the quality of materials used, this bodysuit sports good durability within ambient anomalous fields.\n \n

A sealed case that contains undisclosed contents. It's rather heavy.

A set of PS5 series body armor with beryllium sputter, acquired through shady means by the Zone's Bandits. Although the suit shows visible signs of irreparable wear and tear, it's still an excellent choice for battle.\n \n

This outfit comprises military PS5 series body armor with beryllium sputter modified for use in the Zone. Compared to a standard PS5-M universal protection suit, this outfit is more ergonomic and protective overall.\n \n

Spetsnaz body armor acquired and repurposed by the Zone's Mercenaries. Provides good physical protection while also being remarkably comfortable to wear. Often worn by Mercenaries who used to work for the SSU.\n \n

This outfit comprises military PS5 series body armor with beryllium sputter modified for use in the Zone. Designed for intense rapid-insertion assault operations, this outfit is standard issue for most Spetsnaz operators in the Zone.\n \n

Admin Upgrade kit for the CAR-15. Updates the Classic rifle to bring it more in line with modern M4 style rifles. This kit adds an Aimpoint T2 with G33 Magnifier and replaced the stock and handguard with more contemporary furniture. A LAM and flashlight have also been mounted to the front to assist in target aquisition in dark and light environments.\n \n
Blood Diamon Upgrade kit for the CAR-15. Replicates the look of a classic Hollywood rifle for those that want to live out their Rhodesian role-play fantasy in the zone.\n \n

This kit includes all the parts necessary to convert an MP5 into an MP5k. Parts included are a new upper reciever, hand guard, barrel, and gas system. The rate of fire has been upgraded and the compact size have made it ideal for concealing and carrying as a sidearm. Due to the removal of the stock, the MP5k is no longer able to use scopes effectively.

A higher-magnification spin on the classic PSO formula, the POSP 6x24 was tailored as much for hunters and sport shooters as for military users. Its familiar chevron reticle and stadiametric scale make quick range estimates easy at game-hunting distances, and the slightly longer tube gives cleaner shots on small-to medium-sized targets. Built for export, the POSP found a wide civilian market — prized for its ruggedness, simple controls, and PSO-style ballistic aids that translate neatly from the field to the hunt.

Epinephrine (also known as adrenalin) is a hormone and a neurotransmitter. Epinephrine has many functions in the body, instantly regulating heart rate, blood vessel and air passage diameters, and metabolic shifts. Epinephrine release is a crucial component of the fight-or-flight response of the sympathetic nervous system. The effects lead to increased blood glucose and fatty acids, providing substrates for instant energy production within cells throughout the body.

The Artefact Application Container (AAC) filters a moderate amount of radiation particles emitted from artefacts. This mechanical aluminium container is the most cost-efficient and thus the most popular way of handling artefacts as it allows the user to harness the power of more potent artefacts. Artefact filter is composed of multiple layers made primarily of lead, with one layer of thorium which has strong radiation shielding properties. This device has been manufactured by scientists residing in military bunkers around the Zone. In case of emergency or if no longer needed, the module can be utilized as a source of quality metal for use in outfit or helmet repairs. Currently unusable. To attach it to the belt, you have to put an artefact inside. The artefact will retain its characteristics with the exception of reduced radiation output. Compared to the IAM, the AAC is capable of filtering out gamma rays. Formed by most artefacts, gamma rays are able to penetrate skin, damage DNA, and in high doses, internal organs by breaking the bonds between atoms in a molecule.

The Artefact Application Module (AAM) filters a significant amount of radiation particles emitted from artefacts. This advanced mechanical module is the most effective way of harnessing the power of most potent artefacts. Artefact filter is composed of numerous layers made of nano-carbon. It is hence lightweight and has strongest radiation shielding properties of all devices. Regarded as a symbol of affluence in the Zone. Most commonly used by the scientists to minimize the harmful effects of artefacts. Designed and manufactured in Ukraine by a government institute which provides equipment for the scientists residing in the Zone of Alienation. In case of emergency or if no longer needed, the module can be utilized as a source of quality metal for use in outfit or helmet repairs. Currently unusable. To attach it to the belt, you have to put an artefact inside. The artefact will retain its characteristics with the exception of reduced radiation output. Compared to the AAC, the AAM is capable of filtering out neutrons. Formed by only the most unstable artefacts, neutrons are able to penetrate skin and, in high doses, damage DNA and internal organs by causing instability to a molecule's atomic nuclei, which may then in turn decay to cause further damage. Even if the neutron is 'safely' absorbed, sufficient exposure will lead to deuterium poisoning.

An unprecedented artifact with remarkable properties. It appears to be of organic origin and is highly symmetrical—an unusual trait for artifacts found in the Zone. It increases the structural strength and elasticity of any material it comes into contact with, and if held to the ear, one could swear they hear indistinct whispering voices.

A lump of wrinkled clay and organic remains. Separating individual pieces of material seems impossible due to the presence of some unknown and incredibly strong binding agent. Some scientists suggest the artifact is held together by gravitational anomalies contained within, though this remains unproven. A Geiger counter produces almost no sound when brought close to this strange artifact.

A compound of several hollow organic formations, this artefact emits a gaseous substance that can neutralise chemical or toxic particles inside the body without harming it. Thus providing a a great level of toxic and chemical protection. 
It also has a constant temperature regardless of its surroundings. This allows it to absorb heat from its vicinity and to create a little pocket around the user which prevents the flames from damaging the body. 
Apart from that Bubble, however, possesses numerous negative properties. For example, it greatly amplifies the effects of psy-waves as well as fields produced by mutants and anomalies. This disadvantage is not to be taken lightly: some stalkers have reported that psy influence was so potent when in possession of a Bubble that they felt their heads cracking from the inside and the only thing they could do was scream in pain and run as far as they could.

Truly, an amazing thing. The Black Stone protects against broadly considered adversity but instead is slowly killing the owner. Some stalkers suspect the artefact is a shard of the Monolith.

Being a rare artefact, little is known about its properties and origins. 
Black Spray shares a similar shape to other sapphire-like artefacts. However, the organic nature of the stem (which shares similar properties to Pin artefacts) suggests that its formed from vascular plants under exposure to dangerous psy-fields and electromagnetic waves. 
Another unique property is the ability to delay the transmission of light that enters it. The said mysterious mechanism is applied to psy-waves surrounding the artefact as well. For this particular reason, Black Spray significantly neutralises psy interferences in a large radius. 
These beneficial properties, however, come at a price. This artefact is extremely dangerous when exposed in toxic environments as it amplifies their effect and increases their potency. When exploring toxic areas, it is hence highly recommended to keep the artefact in your backpack. 
Due to the beautiful blue aura it emits, it is highly sought after by jewellers all over the world.

Another positive effect possessed by this artefact is that it accelerates clotting if worn close to the body. Although it is not recommended to keep this artefact in your possession for longer periods of time as blood clots can be hazardous to your health.

When worn, the artefact is also known to slightly counter the harmful effects of psy-anomalies or psy-waves of some mutants.

Borrowing its name from the myth that camels store water in their humps, camelbak is a pack worn on the back or around the waist of the user, containing a bladder that is filled with water to keep one hydrated. Flexible tube with a mouthpiece facilitates use even in most pressing situations. This model is camouflaged and integrated in the suit to protect the system from damage from any source, be it combat or anomaly. Because the container is made of a high-quality textile, experienced stalkers sometimes re-use scrapped camelbaks for quality materials to patch their field outfits.

It is thus extremely useful in order to minimize the effects of extreme heat in situations requiring additional protection. It emits quick electrical impulses which produce a minuscule vacuum absorbing any excess heat and slightly decreasing the temperature around the user. It does not require water to function. Another feature of the body cooler is its ability to resist electricity to a moderate extent. In case of disassembly of the unit using a toolkit, the metal casing can be reused as outfit repair material.

This artefact is formed in anomalous zones with intense thermal activity. Actively consumes excessive heat and remains cool at all times. 
It will also protect the user against electrical currents. On the other hand, exposure to such environments makes the artefact increase the conductivity due to unknown as of now mechanism. For this reason, one is forced to weigh pros and cons of Crystal when in the vicinity of areas of high conductivity. 
This artefact is also highly sought for by jewellers outside the Zone as its inner core is not radioactive and considered to be of exceptional beauty if polished and refined. Only the first 1 cm of its outer layer emits radiation. 
It is, however, highly advised to proceed with caution when planning to bring the artefact to the market as prolonged exposure to even minimal amounts of radioactive materials can result in death of the user. Covered with very hard layer. Relatively heavy due to high density. 

%c[0,209,172,56] Does NOT degrade over time.

This artefact resembles melted cheese interspersed with sapphire-like stones. Only yellow outer shell of the artefact appears to give off radiation and by careful processing it is possible to extract the non-radioactive blue parts for jewellery and other industrial purposes. This is due to the fact that if cleaned properly and polished, sapphire comparison becomes much more explicit: the stones are as hard and durable as this beautiful gem. This quality of theirs and their appearance make them perfect gemstones and jewellers all over the world strive to have them in stock. 
The usefulness of the artefact, however, is not limited to its industrial applications. There is scientific evidence suggesting that sapphire-like stones effectively protect from psy-waves, whereas the outer shell slightly increases resistance to toxicity. 
However, it can cause objects in its vicinity to heat up and in extreme cases to ignite along with the artefact. For this reason, it is safe to conclude that it absorbs heat and is able to convey it to other objects. 
Due to its density the weight of artefact has increased beyond normal standards, and thus it is one of the heaviest anomalous creations in the Zone. 

%c[0,209,172,56] Does NOT degrade over time.

"Big, red and cursed." 
This is how the locals prefer to describe the Death Lamp, while some refer to it as the "Seed of Satan". Many traders believe the rumours surrounding the artefact, thus refusing to buy it. Little is known about its origins, and nothing of value has been discovered so far. 
Death Lamp was first discovered few years ago by a group of three experienced stalkers. Shortly after delivering the artefact to the Ecologists in Yantar, two of them found dead with a horrific expression on their faces. Nothing is known about the fate of the third stalker. 
Following the accident, members of the science team who studied the artefact had reported having nightmare disorder in the days to follow. 
Apart from the mysterious effects, the artefact drains vitality and willpower from the user, often on such a scale that it induces dizziness and in extreme cases causes the user to faint.

Shell is composed of two disk of bluish colour connected by semitransparent material of unknown nature. 
This strange artefact was considered devoid of any useful properties by many scientists for a long time. Only recently has it been discovered that it is indeed quite beneficial. 
When the artefact comes into contact with the body, it will make one feel rejuvenated and thus it increases stamina recovery. This effect has in case of some stalkers induced hyperactivity. One downside of this artefact, however, is that it reduces the number of platelets in blood, thus increasing bleeding rate. 
It provides a considerable level of psy protection and absorbs electromagnetic interference, acting as a grounding material. 
It will catch fire when exposed to open flame or even spontaneously combust. Caution must be taken when approaching heat sources with this artefact. 
Shell is extremely light and very portable.

Shaped like a DNA helix, Mama's Beads are created in the Burner anomaly. One of their most characteristic features is that they constantly pulsate, generating emissions beneficial to the human body. Mama's Beads also possess potent protective qualities: they are able to shield the body and objects from extreme heat.

The weight is also quite high for such a small artefact, which leads to a conclusion that is of high density.

This artefact, which resembles the human eye, considerably increases metabolism of the body, helping wounds heal quicker. Experienced stalkers say that Eye also brings luck. Irrespective of the validity of that statement, its positive effects are indeed quite potent.

Although the system is built of light aluminium, some parts can still prove useful for minor outfit repairs.

A relatively rare artefact forming in chemical areas, Kolobok is highly valued for its ability to heal most kinds of wounds and stop bleeding in a matter of minutes. 
For unknown reasons, scientists are spreading rumours among stalkers that the artefact modifies bearer's genetic code, which is unproven and should be regarded as conscious attempt at monopolizing the supply of the artefact by the scientific community. This does not, however, mean that it is completely harmless. 
One drawback of this artefact is the fact that it will interact to radioactive particles, so it is best to avoid radiation fields when in possession of the artefact, especially since its negative effects are not balanced with positive ones. It will also reduce energy recovery, which is the result of its sharp needle-like appendages. The needles cause small and quickly healing wounds which bring constant pain and itch, thus reducing stamina. 
This artefact also weakens protection against heat and acids due to its high flammability. 
Lightweight and not too much of a burden to carry around.

The Improvised Application Module (IAM) filters a small amount of radiation particles emitted from artefacts. This mechanical metal pouch is the cheapest device that allows the user to harness the power of less potent artefacts. Artefact filter is composed of multiple layers made primarily of lead which shield from radiation. A low-cost alternative to the more reliable AAC and AAM. In case of emergency or if no longer needed, the module can be utilized as a source of quality metal for use in outfit or helmet repairs. Currently unusable. To attach it to the belt, you have to put an artefact inside. The artefact will retain its characteristics with the exception of reduced radiation output. Due to its low quality, the IAM is only able to block beta particles. Formed by all artefacts, beta particles are able to damage skin in a similar way to a burn.

Also colloquially known as "Radioactive Snowflake". At first glance, this artefact resembles a white Kolobok. 
Thanks to its unique properties, the artefact can significantly increase the bearer's muscle tone, thus greatly improving stamina recovery. Due to its high radioactivity, stalkers in possession of it are sometimes jokingly referred to as "Radioactive Snowflakes". 
When faced with the decision whether to carry it on the belt during a mission, you have to carefully weigh its pros and cons: does it pay to have greater endurance and high protection against electricity at the cost of being constantly exposed to its deadly radiation? Snowflake, however, holds a big downside: it interacts with surrounding energy sources by absorbing and amplifying their effects. For this reason, approaching heat sources, toxic areas and psy-fields while holding this artefact is a suicide. 
It is as light as a snowflake and very easy to carry around.

Nightstar will also absorb toxic and chemical agents from the environment, which makes it a valuable artefact for artefact hunters, although the amount of toxicity absorbed is negligible. It is, however, relatively cheap and even stalkers with pretty tight budgets can afford it if opportunity to buy it arises.

A small albeit advanced device that absorbs electrical energy from its closest surroundings. It serves as a protection of sorts as it limits the amount of electricity that comes into direct contact with the body by either blocking it or by shorting it to ground. As it is in fact a very sophisticated resistor circuit, the Surge Suppressor greatly increases electrical resistance of the body. The result is that the user is hit by a significantly lower voltage and is able to survive outbursts which would normally be lethal. It is thus of vital importance to have a surge suppressor on your belt when in proximity of electrical anomalies and other sources of electricity in the Zone. As with all metal objects, it can be used as a source of repair material as long as one has appropriate tools and enough skill.

Represents a rather complex electronic circuit of valuable radio components, which greatly increases the electrical resistance of the body. As a result, the body falls under a lower voltage and is able to withstand discharges of electricity that previously led to death. Thus, it is vitally important to have a current-limiting microreactor when it is necessary to approach electric anomalies or other sources of electricity. A substance functioning as a radioprotector, used to weaken the effects of radiation on the body. A set of specialized brushes which can be used to clean the barrel and other parts of a firearm from dirt, sand, moisture, lubricant residues and other foreign materials that have accumulated during use. Such sets are quite commonly used in the Zone in order to keep firearms in a good shape at all times. It is possible to utilize common tools to have a better access to inner mechanisms of a gun or alcohol to more easily dissolve oil residue. Can also be used to better spread gun oil, however a more cost-efficient method used by most experienced stalkers entails the application of animal fat on the brushes as an alternative lubricant. This particular set fits typical handgun calibers.

The IP2 antitoxin is a complex drug comprising several antidotes, including metoclopramidum, pyridoxine, thiamine, riboxin and other pharmaceuticals. The drug is used to inject a range of antibiotics to neutralize natural poisons, together with synthetic poison absorbers, into the bloodstream. IP2 is not particularly popular in the Zone as most stalkers rely on a gas mask and a protective suit. However, there are still those who have a few units of this antidote stashed for emergency situations. The effects of the drug are long-lasting. Take one capsule at a time.

Behavior of "Battery" artifacts under artificial electromagnetic fields

Scientific paper penned by V.R. Solenov and R.R. Romanovich, detailing the behavior and observed effects of Battery artifacts when subject to various strengths of electromagnetic fields. Under certain VPM levels the Battery is observed to emit brief, intense surges of direct current from the artifact, with the effect not having any correlation with the strength of the field. It is very wordy and verbose, and accompanying the text and equations are several graphs, black and white photos, and test logs. At the back of the paper are schematics on how to contain such a Battery artifact to cause it to output more voltage when subject to a positive current. The notebook is stamped with the official seal of the Ukrainian Ministry of Science.

Observations and analysis of "Full Empty" artifact projected gravitational shells

Scientific paper penned by A.N. Perchenov and V.R. Solenov, detailing the observations of the Full Empty artifact's peculiar gravitational effects. According to the document, the space between Empty disks is the consequence of two disks partially distorting spacetime relative to the positions of each other to create a miniature gravitational anomaly. The contents of what the 'full' part of the Full Empty are remain unknown and are speculated to be the visible form of air particles charged with highly excited zero-point energy. When subject to an alternating current of a certain amperage under vacuum conditions, the contents temporarily dissipate and the gravitational anomaly contained between the two disks expands to create a nearly impervious 'shell' where any solid objects entering the space are caught and deflected from their original trajectory. The discovery of this behavior supposedly injured four interns and two lab assistants when the correct configuration was dialed in. Within the experiment log is a diagram of the vacuum containment device required to replicate this behavior.

Application of 'Flash' artifact fields to engine power output

Scientific paper penned by R.R. Romanovich and A.N. Perchenov, detailing the strange effects that Flash artifacts have on electrically-powered machinery. When a Flash artifact is subjected to a positive electric current in vacuum, the artifact projects a variously sized anomalous field that vastly increases the physical output of electrical machinery in the area - motors run faster, CPUs overclock, and even mechanical watches move faster. Various experimental logs note the propensity of the machines to burn themselves out due to high heat and friction generated if the field is sustained for a prolonged period of time, and most of the paper is otherwise speculation at what could cause such a behavior. The latest prevailing theory is a quantum level influence increasing the flow of electricity and strength of magnetism. However, such theories are unconfirmed. In the middle of the paper is a diagram detailing the containment chamber construction of the artifact used in experimentation.

A high quality curtain helmet made by the same Defense Research Institute as the SSP-99 and SEVA bodysuits. Allows for complete isolation from the conditions of an anomalous environment. A closed-cycle air filtration system supplies the wearer with consistently cool, clean air.\n \n

A basic plastic and rubber respirator, used to protect the wearer from inhaling harmful dust, fumes, vapors or gases. Widely used by rookies due to its light weight and economical environmental protection. However, it provides negligible physical protection and is guaranteed to fail in more heavily irradiated areas.\n \n

An ShMB gas mask refitted with external 40mm filters for greater functionality. As a general rule, masks like this offer respectable protection in areas of increased radioactivity but suffer from a high failure rate in toxic environments.\n \n

Originally built with the intention to protect the Soviet people from radioactive fallout. The filters of this GP-5 have been restored with a great deal of care. Compared to gas mask kits of similar price and availability, this reconditioned respiratory device boasts greater protection against radiation, but an even greater failure rate in toxic environments.\n \n
A regular black fabric balaclava. Stalkers will often drape it around their mouth and nose to avoid breathing in toxic or radioactive particles, but this protection is unreliable at best.\n \n

A modern, high-quality helmet with a built-in air filtration system based on Western models. The design consists of several layers of Kevlar and composite dielectric materials. Most commonly used by the Zone's Spetsnaz operators, especially in conjunction with the PS5 body armor series.\n \n

This focused kit is designed specifically for repairing helmets, gas masks, and other vital headgear used in the Zone. It contains lens polish, strap replacements, sealant for air filters, and minor plating patches to ensure proper protection against environmental hazards.

 %c[d_orange] • %c[pda_white] Compatibility: %c[ui_gray_2] Medium & Heavy Headgear Sets

Bloodsuckers as a species evolved from humans and this affinity can still be seen in the texture of their skin. What is most characteristic, the skin is very soft and pleasant in touch, almost like the skin of a child. This applies, however, only to the outer layer - underneath, bloodsuckers boast well-developed muscles which can be utilized to form a coat of sorts with considerable damage reducing potential, especially in regard to melee attacks. This makes bloodsucker skin a popular choice among experienced hunters who also wear it as a sign of their proficiency. The most unique property and at the same time the main drawback of the skin is its very intense reaction to temperature change - although the tissue itself will only burn in very high temperatures, it can get very hot making it increasingly damaging to wearer and equipment underneath it.
 
 %c[0,255,255,255]PROPERTIES:
 %c[d_orange] • %c[d_orange] only one pelt can be equipped

A hide almost fully covered with a thick layer of fur, removed from one of wild boars found commonly in the Zone. The mutants themselves are quite resistant to damage and can take considerable punishment, and the reason for this is simple: big muscle mass and almost unbreakable bones. The skin, on the other hand, is quite thin (most probably to provide the creature with effective means of thermal regulation), making it a rather poor choice for armor enhancement. The fur in turn makes the hide a relatively potent thermal insulator. Can also be used for light repairs. Unfortunately, wearing the pelt on top of the armor makes the user less resistant to radioactive and toxic damage as heavy particles often accumulate within the fur.
 
 %c[0,255,255,255]PROPERTIES:
 %c[d_orange] • %c[d_orange] only one pelt can be equipped
 %c[d_green] • +2 BR Class
 %c[d_green] • +5% BR Class damage mitigation

Skinning a burer is a lot like skinning a twisted dwarf. Add to it an almost unbearable stench of bad eggs and you get the idea why stalkers consider this quite a feat. Reports indicate that whereas the skin offers relatively low physical protection it can be used to absorb and disperse significant amounts of incoming psy waves when worn over the head. Burer's skin has low electrical resistance, thus the wearer is more susceptible to electrical damage. Furthermore, due to the fact that burers are relatively rarely encountered in the Zone, the skin has significant research potential, making it a valuable trading commodity for the scientists.
 
 %c[0,255,255,255]PROPERTIES:
 %c[d_orange] • %c[d_orange] only one pelt can be equipped

Chimera, being one of the most dangerous mutants in the Zone, is not only a significant threat, but can also be regarded as a viable source of wealth. All parts that can be taken from chimera's corpse can be sold for a large amount of money, but it is the skin that is one of the most valued scientific specimens as well as one of the best armor enhancements available in the Exclusion Zone. It offers very good protection properties and, even more importantly, is relatively light-weight thanks to which it does not limit mobility. However, its most unique feature is that organic fibers found in the skin act as very effective electrical conductors. This fact to a large degree confirms reports of recon units (especially those scouting around the AMK Industrial Complex) concerning sightings of chimeras surrounded in what looked like an electrical anomaly, producing discharges on contact with any hostile living organism. Unfortunately, no unit was able to make it back, so there is no evidence confirming the existence of such a creature.
 
 %c[0,255,255,255]PROPERTIES:
 %c[d_orange] • %c[d_orange] only one pelt can be equipped

As is the case with other humanoid mutants, it's quite an emotional challenge to scalp a controller and it proves to be even more difficult as the face of a controller retains certain characteristics of the person it once was. Confidential sources within the military also revealed that fingerprints of the person who turned into a controller can sometimes be found in the database. Recent analysis shows that not only the hand of the controller is capable of producing psy waves - the whole body is the source of emissions. For this reason, the scalp - when prepared properly - has the ability to absorb and filter psy energy, but for this effect to take place it is necessary to cover your head with multiple scalps. The skin is also electrically conductive. As regards its physical properties, it is light and can only be used for minor outfit repairs. As is the case with all humanoid psy creatures, the skin of a controller is electrically conductive.
 
 %c[0,255,255,255]PROPERTIES:
 %c[d_orange] • %c[d_orange] only one pelt can be equipped

Hide removed from a breed of mutated pigs commonly referred to as fleshes. Its heavy weight (its main drawback) results from a thick layer of fat underneath the relatively soft and shallow skin. It offers low physical protection, but on the other hand considerably reduces the harmful effects of chemical, thermal and electrical anomalies. Can be used only for minor repairs as the layer of fat is useless for the purposes of armor maintenance and must be cut off, leaving only a small patch of shallow skin tissue.
 
 %c[0,255,255,255]PROPERTIES:

A thick hide almost fully covered with a layer of fur, removed from a lurker. The mutants themselves are quite resistant to damage and can take considerable punishment, and the reason for this is simple: big muscle mass and almost unbreakable bones. The skin of lurker is known for being thick and durable, making it a good choice for armor enhancement. The fur in turn makes the hide a relatively potent thermal insulator. Can also be used for light repairs.
 
 %c[0,255,255,255]PROPERTIES:

Densely covered with short and stiff fur, the pelt of pseudodog remains one of the most useful, widespread and versatile mutant pelts available in the Zone. It can be easily attached to armor with even lightest of tools, and yet it's quite durable, which makes it suitable for medium-grade outfit repairs. When used as a whole - it can cover significant area of the body with protective layer which increases resistance to both physical damage and heat. The fur also provides effective insulation. However, as is the case with all such pelts, it easily catches radioactive and toxic particles, so it is highly advised to remove it when in radioactive or toxic areas.
 
 %c[0,255,255,255]PROPERTIES:
 %c[d_orange] • %c[d_orange] only one pelt can be equipped

The skin of a pseudogiant in many ways resembles the skin of an elephant. It's thick and hard in touch, its texture is very irregular and full of folds which act as the creature's cooling mechanism. The average thickness of pseudogiant's skin is around 2 cm, which makes it very durable and capable of withstanding significant damage. This is, however, at the cost of increased weight. Since pseudogiants are known to often live in areas of high toxicity and radioactivity, the hide naturally adapted to such conditions, which further increases its usefulness. It can also be used to patch even the heaviest of outfits but this process requires the most durable and expensive tools available. It is valued not only for its practical qualities, but can also serve as a prestigious hunting trophy and a rare specimen for the scientists in the Zone.

The fur of a psy dog is undoubtedly one of the most beautiful and rare clothing materials in the world. Usually snow white or grey, it is in great demand and can be sold for a hefty sum of money. It's probably made its way into fashion industry thanks to smugglers some of whom have recently turned to hunting mutants. For those in the Zone, it can serve as a blanket, especially during cold nights, and offers efficient rupture protection and mild psy influence reduction when head part of the fur is used as a mask or a hood. However, it easily catches radioactive and toxic particles, so it is highly advised to remove it when in radioactive or toxic areas. Hunters wear it with pride, but only if the creature was slain by their own hands.

A strange and high-tech device created by Professor Sakharov. The Artefacts Melter (AM) allows the manipulation of artefact materials. This tool is fueled using the energy held by artefacts, which can be acquired and stored for further use by melting them down. The AM can also strengthen artefacts of the same kind, or even fuse artefacts of different kinds to generate new ones. The chemical and physical processes at work are not fully understood yet, though recent research points towards the tool's ability to harness the Noosphere's power. These findings quickly prompted most high ranked stalkers to acquire such a tool in order to create stronger and more expensive artefacts for the black market or their own personal use.
 
 %c[0,255,255,255]PROPERTIES:
 %c[d_cyan] • %c[0,255,255,255] Artefact Melting:%c[ui_gray_2] Hold the secure key (Left-ALT) and move the Artefact Melter over any artefact to melt it and refill the AM tank. The more unique the artefact, the more power will be stored.
 %c[d_cyan] • %c[0,255,255,255] Artefact Empowering:%c[ui_gray_2] Choose "Empower artifacts" in the AMOS main menu to combine artefacts of the same kind to strengthen them.
 %c[d_cyan] • %c[0,255,255,255] Artefact Fusion:%c[ui_gray_2] Choose "fuse artifacts" in the AMOS main menu and open the recipe sub-menu to generate a new artefact from 2 or more artefacts. The resulting artefact will have the average condition of its components. AMOS contains already known and "secure" artefact combinations.
 %c[d_cyan] • %c[0,255,255,255] Note: %c[ui_gray_2]AMOS has built-in security protocols to avoid malfunctions. If the tool refuses to work, be sure that the AM tank holds more than 10% (1 use) of its total capacity.

A pocket-sized electronic device designed to cover the most essential functions of a computer, the PDA is crucial for day-to-day information sharing. It comes pre-installed with a GPS module, providing geolocation and time data to the user. This upgraded version has been significantly enhanced to detect and process the locations of other modules connected to the network, making it highly sought after among stalkers. Unlike standard GPS modules, this one uses a set of codes to unlock special privileges and access additional information from the connected network. This particular device also comes with a pirated version of the IMI KilTrak software.

An iron ampoule designed as field equipment for military field personnel for instant pain reduction. It contains a powerful dose of metamizole mixed with an antihemorrhagic drug. Metamizole is one of the strongest Non-Steroidal Anti-Inflammatory Drugs (NSAIDs), especially effective at easing aches and pains as well as reducing fevers. It is also useful as anti-inflammatory drug when used in higher doses. May cause some delayed side effects such as leucopenia or agranulocytosis, so it should be used with moderation. Injected into an arm or a thigh. Its availability in the Zone is limited due to the difficulty of smuggling the medicine across military cordons.

This kit contains oils, solvents, basic cleaning equipment and specialized tools used for disassembly and maintenance of mechanisms of weapons. It also by standard comes with set of elementary firearm spare parts. The kit is quite heavy, but is irreplaceable when venturing deep into the Zone as it can be used in life-or-death situations to make a firearm usable again. Due to differences in weapon construction this particular set can only be used for disassembly and repair of weapons with handgun calibers. It's also possible to additionally increase the effectiveness of the kit by borrowing certain materials from or combining with other kits during a single maintenance session.

OTs-02 "Kiparis" (Cypress) is a sub-machine gun, developed in 1972 by order of the Ministry of Defence of the USSR by weapon designers from TsKIB COO of Tula. The layout is based on the Czechoslovak sub-machine gun Scorpion Vz.61 (1961 variant). The OTs-02 "Kiparis" automatic mechanism is powered by a free bolt recoil. Shots are fired from closed bolt, which increases the accuracy of fire. To increase stability of the weapon, a folding stock is used, which in the compact position is folded on top of the receiver. In the Zone, this weapon is not uncommon and usually used by bandits and loners who can't afford a better weapon.

A shoulder-fired, break-action, single shot grenade launcher first appearing during the Vietnam War. Recoil is kept low by means of a 'High-Low Propulsion System' where propellant is stored in a high-pressure chamber, allowing much larger projectiles to be launched without the heavy equipment usually needed. When the propellant is ignited, the gases are bled out through vents at reduced pressure to a much larger, low-pressure chamber to push the projectile forward. The high-low system allows the weight of the weapon and its ammunition to be reduced significantly.

The newly adopted Close Quarters Battle rifle made by Izhmash. Developed in response to a growing need made apparent by the FSB, the ShAK-12 was purpose built to house an enormous stopping power ability inside of a compact rifle package. It borrows its 12.7x55mm cartridge from the VSSK Vykhlop sniper rifle and utilizes 20 round magazines. Along with sporting a full array of picatinny attachment rails, it also features a removable carry handle for larger optic mounting options. The short recoil operation of the barrel behaves much in the same way as a Barrett M82 does with the barrel recoiling with the bolt to assist in controllability and cartridge extraction. A weapon like this is sure to make it into any STALKER's wishlist, but few will know what it feels like to own it. Whatever the case, there is no suit in the Zone that can withstand the power this gun deals out. Just make sure you aim well, are close and have a good stash of money, because the ammunition is anything but "cheap and available."

Thanks to the powerful 7.62x25 cartridge and relatively long barrel, the TT boasts high muzzle velocity, penetration, and impressive accuracy, even over long distances.

The Uzi is an open-bolt, blowback-operated submachine gun designed by Major Uziel "Uzi" Gal in the late 1940s.
 It was one of the first weapons to use a telescoping bolt design which allows the magazine to be housed in the pistol grip for a shorter weapon.
 Mainly constructed from stamped sheet metal, the Uzi has relatively few moving parts, making it easy to strip for maintenance or repair.
 It is used throughout the world as a police and special-forces firearm, and has been manufactured by Israel Military Industries, FN Herstal, and other manufacturers.

The Micro Uzi is an closed-bolt, blowback-operated submachine gun first introduced in 1986.
 It is an extremely scaled down version of the Uzi with a similar stock model as the Mini Uzi.
 Featuring reduced barrel length, shorter bolt and increased cyclic rate, the Micro Uzi fires from a closed bolt to improve single-shot accuracy.
 It has seen use in several armed forces and ultimately surpassed by the Uzi Pro in 2010.

The Mini Uzi is an open-bolt, blowback-operated submachine gun first introduced in 1980.
 It is a compact version of the famous Uzi which, despite its small size, retains the robustness and reliability of the original.
 Featuring reduced barrel length and a slightly modified mechanism, the Mini Uzi has a greater automatic rate of fire due to the shorter bolt.
 This version is particularly popular for operations in urban environments and confined spaces.

The Walther WA 2000 is a semi-automatic bullpup sniper rifle produced by the Carl Walther GmbH Sportwaffen company from 1982 until 1988. It was produced in three different calibers. Production of the rifle was limited and was shortly stopped because it was too expensive to achieve widespread sales and not robust enough for military use as a sniper rifle. Only 176 were built, making it one of the rarest and most sought out production firearms ever made.

The Winchester 1873 lever-action rifle, often referred to as the "Gun that Won the West," is a historic and iconic firearm introduced in 1873 by the Winchester Repeating Arms Company. Known for its reliability and innovation, this rifle played a significant role in the American frontier and beyond. Chambered in various popular calibers of the time, such as .44-40 and .38-40, it featured a distinctive lever mechanism that allowed for rapid and efficient reloading, making it a favorite among cowboys, lawmen, and outlaws alike. Its elegant design, smooth action, and remarkable durability contributed to its legendary status, making it a prized collector's item today. The Winchester 1873 lever-action rifle symbolizes the spirit of the Wild West and remains an enduring symbol of American firearm history. Adapted to use both .357 ammunition and a modern scope for easier access. \n \n

A lightweight assault rifle system sharing design and engineering with the G36 rifle originally part of the Objective Individual Combat Weapon program for development of a "smart" grenade launcher system with an underslung carbine rifle. Unable to meet performance and weight requirements, the program was eventually canceled and separated, resulting in the XM8 project.

A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. The suit's improved protection against ballistics has been achieved through an unnamed home-made anomalous compound.\n \n
A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. For a stalker suit, the "Drought" possesses above-average ballistic protection, but is considerably less comfortable to wear.\n \n

A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. It was independently designed by a few optimistic stalkers who were expecting to take measurements of psy-storms out in the open. A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. The Sunrise is one of the oldest stalker suits in existence. It is also widely considered the bare minimum protection a stalker should have for serious trips into the Zone.\n \n

A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. An overall upgrade over the "Drought" suit and significantly more comfortable to wear. The "Predator" enjoys popularity among greenhorns and experts alike thanks to its low maintenance costs and great modification potential.\n \n

A stalker bodysuit, specially designed to be fitted with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances. A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. An economical choice with great upgrade potential. Ironically, for being dubbed the "Salamander", it demonstrates a poor ability to safely channel heat.\n \n

A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. One of the heavier stalker suits available, this outfit was designed for those who prefer to make their money as hired killers or fighters instead of looting artefacts or skinning mutants.\n \n

This somewhat outdated hacking device belongs to Stitch.
 It should be compatible with equally outdated pieces of technology scattered across the Zone.

An excellent bodysuit produced by Freedom’s finest artisans consisting of a steel-reinforced Kevlar jumpsuit and a tactical vest with Class IV inserts, all carried by powered limbs and servomotors. While not always the most practical suit to maintain, it is undeniably powerful, and as such is entrusted only to Freedom's finest warriors and guardsmen.\n \n

Also known as the "Radsuit", this outfit is essentially a heavy ballistic vest containing Class IV plates reinforced with steel with many of the attachment points needed for a rigid exoskeleton. However, with no powered exoskeleton to ease the load, this armor proves cumbersome to wear for extended periods of time. Only the most respected members of Freedom will ever be found wearing a suit like this.\n \n

An aged, slightly heavier stalker bodysuit made long ago by Freedom craftsmen. Although the suit has obviously acquired some wear and tear in places, it's roughly on par with most other basic stalker suits, even today. Younger Freedom members will pay through the nose to acquire one of these outfits, simply because of the amazing stories these venerable suits have acquired over the years.\n \n

A set of Spetsnaz body armor that has been "liberated" for use in the Freedom faction. This suit has been treated with "Sovereign", an anomalous compound invented by the faction. Any Freedom member expected to help defend the Barrier will do their best to get their hands on this suit.\n \n
A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by Freedom craftsmen. This comfortable bodysuit has been treated with a special solution known as 'Horizon', to increase resistance to chemical anomalies.\n \n

A Wind of Freedom bodysuit, specially designed to be fitted with the servos and batteries of an exoskeleton, allowing the wearer to carry significantly more equipment than they could under any other circumstances. Compared to a full exoskeleton, it provides less protection but is also considerably lighter.\n \n

A camouflaged leather anorak worn over a turtleneck sweater, tucked into jeans and combat boots. Such attire is typically only worn by Freedom members. An inadequate choice for the Zone's harsh conditions.\n \n

A suit of "Sentinel" body armor specially produced by Freedom craftsmen for observing anomalous phenomena in the Zone. The specially treated materials provide good protection and suit durability during prolonged exploration of anomalous fields.\n \n

A Zone-assembled suit, handmade by local craftsmen. This particular outfit is of high quality and is intended for extended exploration within the Zone. A suit of choice for many artefact and mutant hunters.\n \n

A long, thick overcoat worn over a butchered scientist suit. Although the closed-cycle system and several other features seem to have been stripped off, the end result still compares favorably to many basic stalker suits.\n \n

A long, thick green overcoat worn over a butchered scientist suit. Although the closed-cycle system and several other features seem to have been stripped off, the end result still compares favorably to many basic stalker suits.\n \n
